prose:
Method provenance block
• Property: HOMO and LUMO orbital energies and the HOMO–LUMO gap (eV).
• Level of theory: DFT, tier ‘standard’ → B3LYP/def2-TZVP (RI density-fitted) in PySCF.
• Geometry provenance: single-point calculation on the supplied file /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/benzene/benzene.xyz (no further optimisation). This is therefore a B3LYP/def2-TZVP//input-geometry result.
• Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit solvation).
• Charge = 0, multiplicity = 1 (closed-shell singlet).
• Software/back-end: PySCF via chemkit frontier-orbitals skill.
• Convergence & diagnostics: SCF converged (8 cycles). chemkit integrity check status = ok, trustworthy = true. No engine warnings were reported.

Key numerical results (gas phase)
• HOMO energy  = −7.0468329 eV
• LUMO energy  = −0.41574728 eV
• HOMO–LUMO gap (headline value) = 6.6310857 eV

Reproducibility
• chemkit command:
  /home/erobles/chem-skills/mcp_server/chemkit_engine/cli.py frontier --method dft --functional b3lyp --basis def2-tzvp /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/benzene/benzene.xyz --out …/live.json
• Result JSON: /home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmpuiq4__2j/live.json
• A live PySCF output log was streamed during the run to the standard chemkit frontier_<timestamp>.out file in the working directory; it can be inspected or tailed for full SCF details.

No warnings were emitted by the chemkit engine (warnings array is empty).
